text:
Try this early Grok Build (anything) beta and let us know what to improve. 

Much appreciated! https://t.co/1GgGEQzv3E
summary: Try this early Grok Build (anything) beta and let us know what to improve. Much appreciated!
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces the early beta release of Grok Build, xAI's AI coding/development tool, and solicits user feedback for product improvement. This demonstrates xAI's strategy of rapid iteration and community-driven product development, following a common SaaS/AI product launch pattern of releasing early versions to gather real-world usage data before broader rollout.
embed_html:
Try this early Grok Build (anything) beta and let us know what to improve.

Much appreciated! https://t.co/1GgGEQzv3E

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 14, 2026

text: Go in with expectations that Grok Build is still beta, but improving almost every day https://t.co/U9xG8LCPtQ
summary: Go in with expectations that Grok Build is still beta, but improving almost every day
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that Grok Build (xAI's AI product development tool) remains in beta but is experiencing rapid daily improvements, setting realistic expectations for users about the product's maturity level. This relates to xAI's product development strategy and the iterative approach to launching AI tools—a key lesson in shipping early-stage products while transparently communicating limitations and improvement trajectory to users.

text: @PTrubey Yes, SpaceX deliberately accepted lower revenue deals with airlines in exchange for making Starlink super easy to use and available to all passengers
summary: @PTrubey Yes, SpaceX deliberately accepted lower revenue deals with airlines in exchange for making Starlink super easy to use and available to all passengers

parent.text: Wow. Huge respect. WiFi portals are some of the most annoying things. Starlink would rather give up significant revenue than allow an airline to burden flight customers with portals.
parent.name: Phil Trubey
parent.embed_html:
Wow. Huge respect. WiFi portals are some of the most annoying things. Starlink would rather give up significant revenue than allow an airline to burden flight customers with portals. https://t.co/3tmtR6P4NK

— Phil Trubey (@PTrubey) May 14, 2026

ai_summary: SpaceX's Starlink deliberately structured airline partnership deals at lower revenue terms to eliminate WiFi portal friction and ensure seamless connectivity for all passengers, prioritizing user experience over short-term monetization. This reflects a strategic business decision to build customer goodwill and market adoption by removing barriers to service adoption, demonstrating how trading immediate revenue can establish competitive advantages and customer loyalty in emerging markets.
embed_html:
Yes, SpaceX deliberately accepted lower revenue deals with airlines in exchange for making Starlink super easy to use and available to all passengers

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 14, 2026

text: @SawyerMerritt They will lose passengers over this
summary: @SawyerMerritt They will lose passengers over this

parent.text: Delta claiming Amazon’s LEO offers the “fastest and most cost-effective technology available” is a wild statement considering it won’t even be available on Delta planes until 2028. They could’ve started offering Starlink - an already proven, operational technology - this year...
parent.name: Sawyer Merritt
parent.embed_html:
Delta claiming Amazon’s LEO offers the “fastest and most cost-effective technology available” is a wild statement considering it won’t even be available on Delta planes until 2028. They could’ve started offering Starlink - an already proven, operational technology - this year... https://t.co/vVLK3hjesW

— Sawyer Merritt (@SawyerMerritt) May 14, 2026

ai_summary: The thread discusses Delta Airlines' strategic decision to wait until 2028 for Amazon's LEO satellite internet instead of adopting Starlink's already-operational service, with Elon Musk's insight that this delay risks losing price-sensitive passengers to competitors. This reflects a key business lesson about first-mover advantage and the competitive risk of choosing unproven future technology over established, working solutions in customer-facing services.
embed_html:
They will lose passengers over this

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 15, 2026

parent.text: 🚨 JENSEN HUANG: “The amount of energy that we need for computing is probably 1,000x more than we currently have.”
parent.name: Chief Nerd
parent.embed_html:
🚨 JENSEN HUANG: “The amount of energy that we need for computing is probably 1,000x more than we currently have.” pic.twitter.com/3F2DJ48Ebq

— Chief Nerd (@TheChiefNerd) May 14, 2026

ai_summary: Jensen Huang (NVIDIA CEO) highlights a critical infrastructure challenge: computing energy demands could increase 1,000x beyond current capacity to support AI growth. Elon Musk's response suggests space-based energy solutions (likely referencing solar power satellites or similar SpaceX initiatives) as a potential answer to this massive energy constraint. This thread surfaces a fundamental business bottleneck for AI scaling and points to emerging infrastructure opportunities at the intersection of energy, space technology, and compute—directly relevant to companies like Tesla (energy), SpaceX (space infrastructure), and xAI (compute demands).
embed_html:
Space is the only way

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 15, 2026
